direction.audience_fit: Hackathon, technical, strategy and proposal decks that need a sharp light visual system without cream or parchment tones.

typography.heading: Aptos Display, Inter, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
typography.body: Aptos, Inter, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
typography.mono: SF Mono, Cascadia Code, Consolas, monospace

patterns.title_slide: One-line cobalt title on a light graph grid, with a short proof subtitle and a thin rule.
patterns.section_slide: Large assertion with one cobalt axis line and a small evidence tag.
patterns.comparison_slide: Two or three aligned grid panels with equal heights and short labels.
patterns.table_slide: Hairline table with cobalt column emphasis, generous row height and protected source lane.
patterns.code_slide: White code or architecture block on graph canvas with mono labels and no decorative clutter.
patterns.architecture_slide: Stepped flow, system map or timeline aligned to grid intersections.

avoid: cream, beige, parchment or bone-paper defaults
grid lines behind dense body copy
pixel-glitch accents near sources or controls
accessibility: do not communicate status by color only
source text remains readable on mobile

decisions.decision: Add a Cobalt Grid light style as a preferred non-cream frontend-slides-inspired lane.
decisions.rationale: The user prefers Cobalt Grid's sharp light technical feel and explicitly disliked cream and bone-paper defaults.
